seoTitle: Find Gross Revenue in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to locate your gross revenue figure in QuickBooks Online using the Profit and Loss report, before expenses are subtracted.
intro: Your gross revenue is your total top-line earnings before any expenses or cost of goods sold get subtracted, and QuickBooks Online surfaces this number directly on the Profit and Loss statement. This guide shows where to look and how to keep the figure handy for regular checks on your business's sales velocity.

steps.heading: Open the Reports section
steps.body: From the left sidebar, click Reports to reach your report library.

steps.heading: Search for Profit and Loss
steps.body: Type Profit and Loss into the search bar and select the standard P&L report.

steps.heading: Set your date range
steps.body: Use the customization ribbon to choose the financial period you want to review.

steps.heading: Choose cash or accrual
steps.body: Toggle between Cash and Accrual accounting methods depending on whether you want to see invoiced revenue or collected cash.

steps.heading: Locate the Total Income row
steps.body: Run the report and scroll to the top Income section to find your Total Income or Gross Sales figure, before any deductions.

faq.question: What's the difference between gross revenue and net income?
faq.answer: Gross revenue is your top-line total before expenses, while net income is what's left after all costs and deductions are subtracted.

faq.question: Should I use cash or accrual to check gross revenue?
faq.answer: Cash basis shows what you've actually collected, while accrual shows what you've invoiced — pick whichever matches how you track performance.

faq.question: Can I save this report setup for quick future checks?
faq.answer: Yes, use Save Customization in the upper right to lock in a shortcut you can return to without resetting filters each time.

faq.question: Can I export the gross revenue figure to a spreadsheet?
faq.answer: Yes, the report's export dropdown lets you download a clean Excel or PDF version of the full ledger.
